author: potuz
content: Apologies for the newbie question but why would a gas reprise affect a hw? Shouldn't they just be signers? I'd understand it can affect contracts and software wallets that cook the tx but I don't understand why the signer would be affected at all

author: sky.cactus
content: Some offline wallets (e.g., mobile device based, or offline computer based) are more feature rich.  Dumb signers like Ledger/Trezor likely aren't going to construct or care about gas limits.

author: sky.cactus
content: Edited my original message to be a bit more clear that in my case it is an "offline wallet", not a "hardware wallet" which I agree tends to mean dumb signers, not more advanced offline tools running inside a TEE on an offline mobile device.

author: funnygiulio
content: I think its fine as long as it is say max a week. Also I mean, for new accounts which has no fund this will be a problem

author: funnygiulio
content: Accessing an account already present is cheaper now. So folks WILL have access to their funds regardless

author: funnygiulio
content: The issue might arise if they want to move it to an unfunded address, maybe some exchanges will have some issues but will fix them speedily I am sure. but oh well. They can figure it out, i think we did worse for the sake of scaling anyways

author: misilva73
content: I am not sure how big "the risk is users losing access to their funds for any amount of time because of a hard fork" is in this case. The hardcoded 21k is only a concerns for transfers to new accounts. So, it should be possible to fund a new account from somewhere else and then send those "locked" funds to the newly created account. It is more work, yes, but they funds are not really locked. Or am I missing something?

author: potuz
content: I think the point being raised is that those "locked funds" will remain locked until the user either 1) recreates the wallet in some other functioning wallet that supports the fork or 2) waits until their wallet upgragdes. In this scenario, there's no option to send locked funds to any other account cause the wallet that is used to make that transfer is not functional.

author: funnygiulio
content: But how can they be locked if cost of moving funds is actually lower and you can move them?

author: funnygiulio
content: I mean to fresh new account? Maybe

author: potuz
content: the wallet cannot even make the tx in this scenario

author: potuz
content: so no way to sign to approve that first move

author: funnygiulio
content: Well yeah, i guess thats okay. There is not really a way around this if you want to scale

author: funnygiulio
content: Devs can update their update their software. Will take as long as someone decides to complain
